reasoning: Without the ability to verify primary results in 2025, we rely on a strong base rate from earlier context. New Jersey leans Democratic, and the Democratic nominee typically has around a 60-65% chance of winning the general election. However, the 2025 primary field was crowded with prominent figures (Gottheimer, Fulop, Baraka, Sweeney). Mikie Sherrill, while a formidable candidate, has roughly a 30-35% chance to win that primary. Multiplying her baseline primary win probability (35%) by the Democratic general election win probability (60%) yields approximately 21%.
